scenario: An ops team sees 50 Incidents across various processes. They want to filter to incidents whose error message contains "Timeout" to triage similar problems together.
question: Which Operate feature fits?

options.text: " Manually scanning 50 incidents is time-consuming and error-prone. A human reviewer may misread or skip entries under time pressure. The Operate incident filter functionality provides substring-based message filtering that surfaces all matching incidents instantly without manual scrolling through the full incident list.

" While CSV export and grep can technically filter by error message content, this creates a multi-step workaround using external tooling. The Operate UI's built-in incident filter applies the same substring search interactively within the interface, providing immediate results without any file export or command-line operations.

Operate does not include text-based filtering for incident error messages, making substring search on error message content unavailable. This is factually incorrect. The Operate user interface includes filter panels on the Processes and Instances views that support filtering by error message content, error type, process definition name, and other criteria. Text substring filtering for incident triage is a built-in Operate capability.

Operate's Incident filter on the Processes or Instances view supports filtering by error message and error type. Apply a "Timeout" substring filter to the error message field to surface only the incidents whose error text contains that term. This instantly narrows 50 incidents down to the subset sharing the Timeout error pattern, enabling efficient group triage.

a.text: Incorrect. Operate's filters include error message/type filtering. Use substring search to triage.

c.text: Incorrect. Built-in filters suffice for substring search.
